worth_it_explanation: DoubleVerify's value depends entirely on where it is activated. A DV contract that covers one DSP while other platforms in the same media plan run unverified creates measurement gaps that are invisible in DV Pinnacle reports because those reports only surface what DV can see. These checks confirm whether DV is integrated across every platform the advertiser buys on, whether pre-bid avoidance is active where post-bid monitoring exists, and whether protection products are applied consistently rather than selectively. The gaps are structural, not campaign-level, and they do not surface through standard performance reporting.
intro: DoubleVerify operates through two distinct integration modes on each demand-side platform (DSP): post-bid monitoring, which measures impression quality after the bid is placed and the ad is served, and pre-bid avoidance, which applies DV's fraud, brand safety, and viewability segments inside the DSP's targeting layer before the bid is made. These two modes serve different functions. Post-bid monitoring surfaces what ran and where. Pre-bid avoidance prevents low-quality inventory from being purchased in the first place. A media plan running across multiple DSPs may have DV integrated on some platforms and not others, or active for post-bid monitoring only on platforms where pre-bid segments are also available. The checks in this subcategory examine whether DV Pinnacle's coverage matches the actual buying footprint.

audit_checks.title: DV is not integrated on every platform where the advertiser buys media
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Every buying platform without a DV integration is a complete measurement blind spot. Fraud, viewability, and brand safety data from unintegrated platforms never reach DV Pinnacle, so the advertiser has no verified quality baseline for that spend. Budget allocations, optimizations, and reporting comparisons across platforms then rest on an uneven measurement foundation where some spend is verified and some is not.

educate.base:
DoubleVerify provides post-bid monitoring and pre-bid avoidance through integrations with individual DSPs. Each integration is a separate relationship. DV Pinnacle reports only on platforms where an active integration exists. If the advertiser buys on five DSPs but DV is integrated on three of them, the remaining two generate spend with no fraud filtration, viewability measurement, or brand safety verification visible to the advertiser through DV.

This gap is not visible from inside DV Pinnacle. The platform accurately reports what it sees. The problem is that what it does not see does not appear as a gap. A campaign that looks well-protected in DV reporting may be running significant budget on platforms that have never been connected.

The way to identify this gap is to compare the advertiser's full buying footprint against the list of platforms where DV is active. The buying footprint includes every DSP, social platform, and retail media network where the advertiser currently runs campaigns. DV's integration list covers its active partnerships.

A few situations make this gap more likely:
  • The account expanded to a new buying platform after the original DV contract was set up, and the integration was never requested for the new platform
  • A media mix includes social platforms or retail media networks that require separate DV activation processes from programmatic DSPs
  • Multiple agency partners or internal teams manage different platforms independently, and DV integration was not part of each team's standard setup
You may want to list every platform currently receiving budget and cross-reference it against the platforms active in DV Pinnacle before concluding that coverage is complete.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For ecommerce advertisers running across retail media networks in addition to programmatic DSPs, you may want to confirm that DV is integrated on the retail media side as well. Retail media networks have distinct integration processes and may not be covered by existing programmatic DV activations.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For lead-generation advertisers buying across multiple DSPs and social platforms, you may want to confirm that every platform receiving budget has a DV integration, as social platforms in particular require platform-specific activation steps separate from programmatic DSP integrations.

learn_more.source_title: MRC standards and guidelines for digital advertising measurement

educate.learn_more_note: DoubleVerify maintains its platform integration documentation, partner lists, and DV Pinnacle setup guides within its partner portal, which requires a client login. Practitioners with DV account access can find current integration documentation and the partner DSP list inside DV Pinnacle.

investigate.intro: Confirm that DV is actively integrated on every platform where the advertiser currently buys media.

dv_pinnacle.label: DoubleVerify Pinnacle
steps: Log in to DV Pinnacle and navigate to the account settings or integration management area. Note every platform listed as active in the account.
In a separate document, list every DSP, social platform, and retail media network where the advertiser currently runs campaigns. Include platforms managed by any agency partners or internal teams.

Compare the two lists. For any platform receiving budget that does not appear in the DV Pinnacle active integration list, note the platform name and the approximate monthly spend running there without verification.

For platforms where DV has a known integration available, flag each unconnected one as a gap requiring activation. For platforms where DV does not yet have an integration, note whether a third-party tag solution or alternative measurement approach is in place.

evaluate.logic: For each platform in the advertiser's buying footprint, the practitioner records whether an active DV integration exists. A platform with a confirmed active integration passes. A platform receiving budget without a DV integration is a fail, recorded with the platform name and the estimated monthly spend running without verification.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record each buying platform and its DV integration status above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s media plan includes buying platforms where DoubleVerify (DV) is not integrated, meaning that spend on those platforms runs with no fraud filtration, viewability measurement, or brand safety verification visible through DV Pinnacle. Platforms without an active DV integration do not appear as gaps in DV reporting; they simply do not appear at all, which makes the coverage gap invisible in standard DV output. {{voice_recommend}} mapping every platform currently receiving budget against the active DV integrations in DV Pinnacle, and requesting activation for each platform where DV supports an integration but none has been established.

audit_checks.title: DV is integrated for post-bid monitoring only, with no pre-bid avoidance segments activated
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Post-bid monitoring records impression quality after the bid is won and the ad is served. Pre-bid avoidance applies DV's segments inside the DSP before the bid, excluding inventory that DV classifies as fraudulent, unsafe, or non-viewable before a dollar is spent. Running post-bid monitoring without pre-bid avoidance means low-quality impressions are purchased and then reported. The advertiser pays for inventory that DV subsequently flags, which is a recoverable efficiency loss on a per-impression basis but compounds across the volume of a programmatic campaign.

educate.base:
When DV is integrated with a DSP for post-bid monitoring, a measurement tag fires on each impression after it is served. DV records whether the impression was viewable, whether human traffic was present, and whether the content environment was brand-safe. This data flows into DV Pinnacle as reported metrics.

Pre-bid avoidance works differently. DV provides segment data to the DSP's targeting layer before bidding. The DSP queries this data as part of its bid evaluation and excludes inventory that DV has classified as high-fraud, low-viewability, or brand-unsafe. The bid is never placed on excluded inventory.

Many DSPs support both integrations. Post-bid monitoring is typically the first integration established because it requires only a tag. Pre-bid avoidance requires the DSP to ingest DV's segment taxonomy and apply it in the bidding logic, which is a distinct integration step that is not automatic when post-bid monitoring is activated.

The practical consequence is that an account running post-bid monitoring without pre-bid avoidance on a platform buys impressions across the full available inventory, then receives a report showing what portion of that inventory failed DV's quality thresholds. The failed impressions were paid for before the measurement surfaced the problem. Pre-bid avoidance prevents those bids from being placed.

A few indicators suggest pre-bid avoidance may not be active:
  • DV Pinnacle shows monitoring data for a platform but the investigate step in DV reveals no pre-bid segment IDs configured in the DSP's targeting
  • The DSP's brand safety or inventory targeting section shows no DV segments applied at the campaign or advertiser level
  • Post-bid DV reports for the platform show sustained fraud or viewability rates higher than what DV reports for other platforms where pre-bid is active
You may want to check the DSP's inventory targeting or brand safety settings alongside DV Pinnacle to confirm whether pre-bid segments are applied.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For ecommerce advertisers where brand adjacency affects purchase intent, you may want to confirm that pre-bid brand safety avoidance segments are active, not just post-bid measurement, so that the advertiser's ads are not appearing in brand-unsafe environments before DV has a chance to report the placement.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For lead-generation advertisers where fraud rates affect cost-per-lead calculations, you may want to confirm that pre-bid fraud avoidance segments are active on every platform since post-bid measurement alone documents fraud after the spend has occurred but does not prevent it.

learn_more.source_title: Enabling DoubleVerify pre-bid segments in Quantcast

educate.learn_more_note: DoubleVerify's documentation on pre-bid segment setup, the list of DSPs that support pre-bid activation, and the process for requesting activation on specific platforms are available within DV Pinnacle to logged-in clients.

investigate.intro: Confirm whether pre-bid avoidance segments are active on each platform where DV post-bid monitoring is running.

dv_pinnacle.label: DoubleVerify Pinnacle
steps: In DV Pinnacle, identify which platforms have active post-bid monitoring configured. Note each platform.
For each of those platforms, navigate to the programmatic settings or segment configuration area in DV Pinnacle. Confirm whether pre-bid segment IDs have been generated and are active.

Log in to each DSP. Navigate to the inventory quality, brand safety, or targeting section at the advertiser or campaign level. Confirm whether DV pre-bid segment IDs appear in the targeting configuration.

For any platform where DV post-bid monitoring is active but no pre-bid segment IDs are applied in the DSP's targeting, record the platform name and the DV products that are available for pre-bid activation on that platform.

evaluate.logic: For each platform with active DV post-bid monitoring, the practitioner records whether pre-bid avoidance segments are also active in the DSP's targeting layer. A platform with both post-bid monitoring and pre-bid avoidance in place passes. A platform where post-bid monitoring runs without pre-bid avoidance, and where the DSP supports pre-bid DV integration, is a fail recorded with the platform name and the DV products available for pre-bid activation.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record each monitored platform and whether pre-bid avoidance segments are applied above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that one or more of {{site_name}}'s buying platforms have DoubleVerify post-bid monitoring active but no pre-bid avoidance segments applied in the DSP's targeting. Post-bid monitoring records impression quality after the bid is won and the ad is served; pre-bid avoidance prevents bids from being placed on inventory that DV classifies as fraudulent, brand-unsafe, or below viewability thresholds. Running monitoring without avoidance means low-quality inventory is purchased and then reported rather than excluded before purchase. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing each monitored platform in DV Pinnacle to confirm whether pre-bid segment IDs have been generated, and working with the DSP and DV account team to activate pre-bid segments on platforms where the integration is available.

audit_checks.title: DV protection products are enabled on some platforms but not others with no documented rationale
audit_checks.impact_rationale: When DV products such as fraud avoidance, viewability pre-bid, or brand safety segments are active on some buying platforms but absent on others covering the same campaign objective, the advertiser is applying unequal quality floors to comparable spend. Performance comparisons across platforms are then measuring different things: one platform's results reflect verified, filtered inventory while another's include unverified impressions. The inconsistency makes optimization and reporting conclusions unreliable.

educate.base:
DoubleVerify offers a set of protection products that can each be activated independently by platform: fraud and invalid traffic (IVT) avoidance, viewability pre-bid targeting, brand safety avoidance, and supply refinement segments. When DV is integrated on multiple DSPs, each product can be in a different activation state on each DSP. Fraud avoidance might be active on DSP A but not on DSP B. Viewability pre-bid might be active on DSP A and DSP B but absent on DSP C. This is not a platform limitation; it reflects how each integration was set up.

Inconsistent activation creates a quality floor problem. A campaign running the same creative across three DSPs with different DV configurations is not running equivalent campaigns. The DSP with full DV pre-bid coverage has a higher quality floor than the one running without it. When the advertiser reviews performance across platforms, the DSP with stronger DV coverage may show better quality metrics not because its inventory is inherently better but because more low-quality inventory was excluded before the bid was placed. Attributing the difference to platform quality rather than protection configuration leads to allocation decisions that are based on a measurement artifact.

Inconsistency is also a risk exposure question. A brand safety avoidance segment that is active on one DSP but absent on another means the advertiser's ads may appear in environments the brand has explicitly excluded on the first platform because the second platform has no record of those exclusions.

You may want to document the activation state of each DV product on each buying platform and assess whether differences are intentional, based on platform-specific factors, or are gaps from setup that was never completed.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For ecommerce advertisers running prospecting and retargeting across multiple DSPs, you may want to confirm that viewability pre-bid and fraud avoidance are applied consistently across all platforms. Inconsistent floors create asymmetric quality conditions that make cross-platform return-on-ad-spend (ROAS) comparisons unreliable.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For lead-generation advertisers comparing cost-per-lead across DSPs, you may want to confirm that DV fraud avoidance is applied consistently on all platforms since a platform without fraud protection may show a lower cost-per-lead that reflects unfiltered invalid traffic rather than genuine lead quality.

learn_more.source_title: MRC standards and guidelines for digital advertising measurement

educate.learn_more_note: DoubleVerify's documentation on available products per DSP, segment configuration options, and the process for auditing activation state across platforms is available within DV Pinnacle to logged-in clients. The DV account team can provide a current list of which products are available for activation on each integrated platform.

investigate.intro: Map the activation state of each DV product across every integrated buying platform to identify inconsistencies.

dv_pinnacle.label: DoubleVerify Pinnacle
steps: In DV Pinnacle, navigate to the programmatic or segment configuration area and list every active platform integration. For each platform, note which DV products are currently active: fraud avoidance, viewability pre-bid, brand safety pre-bid, and supply refinement.
Create a matrix with buying platforms as columns and DV products as rows. Mark each cell as active, inactive, or unavailable based on what you find in DV Pinnacle.

For any DV product that is active on at least one platform but inactive on another where it is available, note the gap. Confirm with the account or the DV team whether the gap is intentional, such as a platform-specific exclusion based on inventory type, or is an omission from setup.

Review DV Pinnacle's post-bid quality reports by platform. If a platform with fewer active DV products shows substantially higher fraud, lower viewability, or more brand safety flags than platforms with full DV activation, the metric gap may reflect the protection difference rather than underlying inventory quality.

evaluate.logic: For each DV product available across integrated platforms, the practitioner records which platforms have it active and which do not. Platforms missing a product that is available and active on comparable platforms are flagged unless a documented rationale exists for the difference. The practitioner notes each gap with the platform name, the absent product, and whether the difference is intentional or undocumented.
evaluate.fallback_message: Complete the platform-by-product matrix above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that DoubleVerify protection products are not applied consistently across {{site_name}}'s integrated buying platforms. Some platforms have fraud avoidance, viewability pre-bid, or brand safety segments active while others running comparable campaigns do not, without documented rationale for the difference. This creates an uneven quality floor across the media plan that makes cross-platform performance comparisons unreliable since platforms with more complete DV activation are held to a higher inventory standard than those without it. {{voice_recommend}} mapping the activation state of each DV product across all integrated platforms, identifying gaps that are not justified by platform-specific inventory considerations, and requesting activation of missing products where they are available.
